summary: smart_grep missing cacheable:true causes unnecessary cache invalidation on every read-only grep
detail: smart_grep is a pure read operation (shells out to rg) but lacks cacheable:true in its tool definition. The write-side invalidation in session/tools.ts fires on any tool where cacheable is falsy, so every smart_grep call flushes the entire read cache. Fix: add cacheable: true (or cacheable: { ttl: "3 minutes", maxSize: 256 * 1024 }) to match the grep tool's configuration.

summary: cache-pipeline.test.ts mislabeled — tests cache layer not pipeline; missing COND-005(b+c) tests
detail: The test file exercises ToolCache.Service directly via testEffect(ToolCache.defaultLayer), not the pipeline wired into tool.ts wrap(). COND-005 explicitly requires tests for (b) permission bypass on cache hit and (c) abort signal interaction with cached results — both require the full pipeline layer. Additionally, no TTL/expiry test exists despite TestClock being available via the testEffect helper.

summary: MCP write tools do not trigger cache invalidation — stale reads possible after MCP mutations
detail: The cache invalidation block (lines 110-125) is inside the builtin tool loop only. MCP tools (lines 160-249) have no invalidation logic. A non-cacheable MCP write tool (e.g., filesystem write) would leave the built-in read cache intact, producing stale cached results on subsequent reads. Fix: add the same invalidation block with span annotations to the MCP tool execution path for non-cacheable MCP tools.

summary: Missing miss_reason: "not_found" annotation on cold cache miss path
detail: When hitResult.kind === "miss" (entry genuinely not in cache), the code falls through to the compute path which annotates result_source: "compute" but no miss_reason. Downstream observability consumers cannot distinguish "computed because not in cache" from "computed because TTL expired" without examining a separate annotation. Fix: add cache.miss_reason: "not_found" to the cold miss branch or the compute annotation site.

summary: core gen block in tool.ts wrap() too large — retry re-runs decode+cache-key derivation
detail: Lines 180-233 combine decode, cache.getOrCompute, execute, and truncate into a single Effect.gen block labeled "core". When Effect.retry fires, it re-runs the entire generator — re-decoding args and re-deriving the cache key on every attempt. For cache-hit scenarios on retry, this is wasted work. Refactoring truncation out of the gen block into its own .pipe() layer after retry would tighten the retry boundary.
